subject: Ecology
title: Overfishing
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Overfishing.

hook.text: In the 1990s, Atlantic cod stocks collapsed after centuries of fishing. They never recovered.

buildup.text: Modern trawlers can locate and catch fish faster than populations can reproduce.

discovery.text: Roughly one-third of global fish stocks are now overfished beyond sustainable limits.

twist.text: Bycatch—unintended species caught in nets—kills millions of sharks, turtles, and dolphins.

climax.text: Marine reserves where fishing is banned show rapid recovery—fish return in just a few years.

punchline.text: The ocean can heal—if we stop taking too much.

quiz.question: What fraction of global fish stocks are overfished?
